phases.notes: Dreamer reads dream aloud. Setup: shopping center, computer talk, falls asleep.
Pierre redirects from interpretation to dream-logic. Identifies pretending as core mechanism, learning goal, and failure outcome.

Four states of mind extracted: (1) pretending interest despite vagueness, (2) revealing truth about falling asleep, (3) worrying about being offensive, (4) dismissing encounter. Naming: creepy, phony, vague, cheerleader. States 2-3 connected.

Pierre traces pattern to childhood. Dreamer recalls age 8 TV contest memory. Mother's prohibition 'you can't do that' identified as origin. Dreamer connects to lifelong pattern of self-suppression. Core belief about self-revelation being offensive traced to mother's reaction.

Pierre notes dreamer is currently violating the old teaching by revealing herself in session. Dreamer expresses relief at having something concrete to reference. Pierre reframes: 'maybe you no longer have to pretend you're not there.'

questions.pg_asks: Asks dreamer to interpret her own dream
Redirects dreamer back to dream content rather than life interpretation
questions.trigger: Dreamer interprets dream as about her general life patterns

questions.pg_asks: Presses for the core problem in the dream
questions.trigger: Dreamer gives surface-level worry about others' opinions

questions.pg_asks: Challenges dreamer to state what she pretends and what follows
Asks what dreamer gets for pretending
Identifies stated goal and asks if it was achieved
Names the strategy as a failure and asks dreamer's reaction
Asks dreamer to grade her own recovery questions
questions.trigger: Dreamer describes her fake questions to the speaker

questions.pg_asks: Asks what kind of information her questions would actually yield
Asks whether dreamer's questions are designed to recover content
Confirms dreamer is pretending and names it as putting on a mask
Contrasts pretending state with truth-telling state
questions.trigger: Dreamer describes telling stranger about falling asleep

questions.pg_asks: Asks about the worry state of mind that follows truth-telling
questions.trigger: Dreamer describes feeling need to not be offensive

questions.pg_asks: Asks what the pretending state was like psychically for the dreamer
questions.trigger: Dreamer admits she wasn't aware of pretending at the time

questions.pg_asks: Asks about the emotional quality of revealing truth
questions.trigger: Dreamer names pretending as creepy and phony

questions.pg_asks: Asks what dismissing the encounter felt like
Revisits truth-telling moment and its emotional consequence
Asks what it feels like to tell someone the truth about what she's going through
Challenges dreamer about the strategy of never revealing truth about oneself
questions.trigger: Dreamer confirms fear of offending through self-revelation

questions.pg_asks: Offers to trace the pattern back to mother
questions.trigger: Dreamer acknowledges the pattern operates her whole life

questions.pg_asks: Asks what it would be like as a child to share feelings with mother
Presses for more detail on mother's reaction to child's self-expression
questions.trigger: Dreamer says mother would get worried and scared

questions.pg_asks: Asks about the effect on dreamer when mother reacted with fear
Asks dreamer to name what mother was doing to her in that moment
Asks what it means that a few words had such lifelong power
Notes dreamer is currently violating the old teaching by revealing herself

beliefs_identified.statement_paraphrase: Self-revelation is offensive to others and should be avoided
beliefs_identified.origin_scene_paraphrase: Mother reacted with fear and prohibition when child (age 8) shared her intention to participate in a TV yelling contest. Mother's words effectively silenced the dreamer's self-expression.
beliefs_identified.deflation_trigger: Recognizing the mother's reaction as the origin rather than a truth about self-revelation. Seeing that she is currently violating the old teaching by revealing herself in session.

beliefs_identified.statement_paraphrase: Pretending to be interested is a viable strategy for social engagement
beliefs_identified.deflation_trigger: Naming the strategy as a failure when the learning goal was not achieved

beliefs_identified.statement_paraphrase: Telling the truth about one's inner experience imposes on others
beliefs_identified.origin_scene_paraphrase: Mother's worried, scared, offended reaction to child's self-expression taught that disclosure burdens others.
beliefs_identified.deflation_trigger: Dreamer notices she is currently doing what the belief prohibits — sharing truth about herself — and it feels different now.

beliefs_identified.statement_paraphrase: Acting on personal desires independently is forbidden
beliefs_identified.origin_scene_paraphrase: Mother's prohibition 'you can't do that' in response to child's independent TV contest participation. Family did not attend to support her.
beliefs_identified.deflation_trigger: Recognizing the contradiction between mother's empowerment messages and prohibition messages

resistance_moments.resolution_method: Pierre redirected back to dream content, separating life interpretation from dream analysis
Pierre held the frame: the strategy is a failure, naming it directly rather than allowing escape
Audience member offered alternative reading; dreamer defended ownership of dream analysis. Pierre gracefully released control of approach momentarily.
Dreamer tried to excuse mother as 'just reacting.' Pierre pressed: what was the effect on you regardless of her intent?

breakthrough_markers.after_state: Emotional recognition: functioning through pretense felt creepy and wasteful
Spontaneous naming: phony, vague, cheerleader — emotional vocabulary emerging
Visceral recognition: feels like imposing on others, wants to apologize and stop talking
Pattern traced to mother's reaction — origin located outside self
Recognition that she lived out mother's prediction — internalized the prohibition
Amazed recognition of how powerful and quick the prohibition was — glad she called before telling
Relief at having a concrete origin to reference — 'I just thought it was something about me'

logos_indicators.context_paraphrase: Dreamer spontaneously names her own functioning as creepy and a waste — no self-protective filtering
Dreamer spontaneously generates emotional vocabulary (phony, vague, cheerleader) without being prompted for specific words
Dreamer accurately names the feeling of self-revelation as imposing on others — appropriate emotional response rather than defended intellectualization
Dreamer spontaneously recognizes the irony and power of mother's prohibition with amusement — ego-free assessment of her own history
Dreamer recognizes the origin pattern and expresses relief without needing to intellectualize or explain it away

notable_patterns: Pierre spent the entire 52-minute session on one dream — unusual depth commitment
Four-states-of-mind extraction method used to map the dream's emotional logic
Origin tracing via direct childhood memory recall rather than symbolic interpretation
Audience member interjected to defend dreamer's questions — dreamer asserted ownership of her own analysis
Integration achieved when Pierre pointed out dreamer is currently violating the old teaching by revealing herself in session
Mother's contradictory messages (empowering stories vs. prohibitive reactions) identified as the structural tension

dream_elements.description_paraphrase: Large shopping center with a man giving a talk against a wall
Man giving a computer talk at a makeshift stand
Asking fake questions to appear interested
Stranger met while leaving the shopping center
Telling the stranger she had fallen asleep
Another person from the event encountered while walking
Walking away and dismissing the whole encounter

scene_structure.description_paraphrase: Dreamer arrives at shopping center talk on computers, stands with others, falls asleep
scene_structure.emotional_peak: Embarrassment upon waking and realizing everyone has left
scene_structure.key_action: Falls asleep during talk she joined hoping to learn something
scene_structure.pg_focus: Yes — used to identify the pretending mechanism and the failed learning goal

scene_structure.description_paraphrase: Dreamer asks fake questions to the speaker after the talk to maintain pretense of having been attentive
scene_structure.emotional_peak: Relief at walking away from the pretense
scene_structure.key_action: Asks superficial questions while pretending to have been present
scene_structure.pg_focus: Yes — used to extract the pretending state of mind and the failure of the recovery strategy

scene_structure.description_paraphrase: Dreamer encounters someone outside the shopping center, asks about the talk, admits she fell asleep
scene_structure.emotional_peak: Anxiety and regret after revealing she fell asleep — fears offense
scene_structure.key_action: Truth-telling moment followed by worry about being offensive
scene_structure.pg_focus: Yes — central to analysis. Used to extract states 2 and 3 (truth-revealing and worrying) and trace to origin

scene_structure.description_paraphrase: Dreamer encounters another person from the event, attempts more social engagement, then dismisses the whole thing
scene_structure.emotional_peak: Dismissal and relief — closure on failed learning quest
scene_structure.key_action: Runs into third person, briefly engages, then walks away
scene_structure.pg_focus: Yes — used to extract the dismissal state and its connection to the learning pattern
